calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Blackman for (administrator) Howard Gelles.

The Court stated that this is a petition for final distribution, that there are problems with the way the estate was administrated, that there should be an assignment for distribution to the PSNT, that when the administrator did not qualify for an increased bond the Court needed to know and that there should have been a request for a deposit of funds into a blocked account. Mr. Blackman stated that the excess bond had been granted, that there was an issue in qualifying for a blocked account in San Diego and that they should have found another administrator. The Court stated that the parties do not get to sidestep the Court. Mr. Blackman stated that it was inadvertence, that they filed a bond, that they thought this was the most effective way without looking for a new administrator, that the assets were protected, that there was $345.00 in interest, that they do not feel that either the administrator or attorney should pay for the loss and apologized for not informing the Court. The Court stated that it was the administrator and attorney who decided what to do and that it is not fair to the beneficiaries. Mr. Blackman stated that the Court has to do whatever it has to do, that they feel the loss should be bourn by all parties but will let the Court decide who to charge it to. Mr. Blackman confirmed with the Court that the National Pooled Trust is acceptable, explained the history of the family/Daniel and that the administrator has not seen Daniel in over 30 years. The Court took the Petition for Final Distribution under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
